Effective DateSection effective , see , set out as a note under .Oct. 1, 1985section 1404 of Pub. L. 98–525section 520b of this title

Privately Funded Abortions at Military HospitalsMemorandum of the President of the United States, , 58 F.R. 6439, provided:Jan. 22, 1993Memorandum for the Secretary of Defenseof the United States Code prohibits the use of Department of Defense (“DOD”) funds to perform abortions except where the life of a woman would be endangered if the fetus were carried to term. By memoranda of , and , DOD has gone beyond what I am informed are the requirements of the statute and has banned all abortions at U.S. military facilities, even where the procedure is privately funded. This ban is unwarranted. Accordingly, I hereby direct that you reverse the ban immediately and permit abortion services to be provided, if paid for entirely with non-DOD funds and in accordance with other relevant DOD policies and procedures.Section 1093 of title 10December 21, 1987June 21, 1988You are hereby authorized and directed to publish this memorandum in the Federal Register.William J. Clinton.

children.heading: .—Restriction on Use of Funds
children.label: a .—Restriction on Use of Funds
children.text: Funds available to the Department of Defense may not be used to perform abortions except where the life of the mother would be endangered if the fetus were carried to term or in a case in which the pregnancy is the result of an act of rape or incest.

children.heading: .—Restriction on Use of Facilities
children.label: b .—Restriction on Use of Facilities
children.text: No medical treatment facility or other facility of the Department of Defense may be used to perform an abortion except where the life of the mother would be endangered if the fetus were carried to term or in a case in which the pregnancy is the result of an act of rape or incest.
